reviews.author: Earth Wanderer
reviews.text: The Best View in Halong Bay – Worth Every Step! Ti Top Island is an absolute must-visit if you are cruising through Halong Bay. It offers a perfect mix of adventure and relaxation in one spot. The Highlights: * The Viewpoint: The climb to the top is about 400–450 stone steps. It’s quite steep and can be a bit tiring in the humidity, but the 360-degree panoramic view of the limestone karsts and emerald water at the summit is breathtaking. It’s easily the best photo spot in the entire bay! * The Beach: After the hike, the crescent-shaped white sand beach at the bottom is perfect for a cooling swim. The water is calm, clear, and very refreshing. * Facilities: There are basic changing rooms, showers, and a small stall where you can grab a cold drink or a coconut. Pro-Tips for Visitors: * Wear proper shoes: Don't try to do the climb in loose flip-flops; sneakers or sturdy sandals with grip are much better. * Bring water: You’ll definitely want some by the time you reach the top! * Time your visit: It can get very crowded when the large cruise boats arrive. If you can, try to head up the stairs immediately to beat the initial rush. Highly recommended for anyone wanting that "postcard" view of Vietnam!

reviews.author: Alexander Ch
reviews.text: Visited Ti Top Island during our Ha Long Bay cruise, and it’s one of the best stops in the area. Ti Top Island offers a sandy beach, swimming area, viewpoint hike, and panoramic observation deck — all in one compact location. The hike to the top viewpoint takes about 10–15 minutes via well-built stairs. No hiking boots needed. Suitable for most visitors, including families, older travelers, and kids if taken at a steady pace. At the summit, you get a 360-degree panoramic view of Ha Long Bay, including limestone karsts, emerald water, and cruise boats — one of the most photographed viewpoints in Vietnam. At the base, there is a clean beach for swimming, plus the well-known Ti Top statue. It’s a classic stop on most Ha Long Bay boat tours and definitely worth including in your itinerary. If you’re searching for the best viewpoint in Ha Long Bay or a short scenic hike with amazing views, this is it. Highly recommended stop during any Ha Long Bay day cruise or overnight cruise.

reviews.author: Agnes Ayu
reviews.text: Indeed, Halong Bay tourism is very interesting. at tip top I didn't climb to the top but just enjoyed the natural scenery on the coast. eating jackfruit and ice cream and relaxing is very enjoyable. The water is very clear, the place is spacious and the view can make life's problems disappear instantly

reviews.author: Ashesh Bharadwaj
reviews.text: This was part of the Halong Bay day cruise. The beach here is small but picturesque surrounded by limestone formations. The walk to the top is slightly difficult but the views are fantastic. You can climb as much as you can if not the whole way and still get to see beautiful views

reviews.author: Z M
reviews.text: Another must visit spot in Ha Long bay. Few steep steps to the top, nothing too challenging. People of all Ages were doing it. Once you make it up top you'll get these incredible views over the bay.

reviews.author: elby
reviews.text: Interesting first island stop on our tour of the bay. The hike up is a little challenging but nothing too difficult, however the problem is with people cutting the queue on the way up or down which contributes to congestion. Its pretty much one way up and the same way down so I hope people can be a little more civilized. Once at the top you have lovely panoramic views all around which makes it worth it. There's a nice small stretch of sand beach too that's pretty nice.

reviews.author: PARTHA BHATTACHARJEE
reviews.text: A small beach to enjoy the bay waters and to climb atop via stair cases to the view point. However, the climb, although staired, is steep in gradient with a bit high steps - so not recommended for everybody. I couldn't make it to the top for factors like paucity of time and limitation in physical fitness. Could have completed the activity with more time and rest but 45 minutes is too short for most of us 60+ Indian citizens. However, I didn't climb upto the 2nd stage view and rest point and the view of Halong Bay from that point is gorgeous. Despite difficulty, its highly recommended for physically fit persons with adequate time. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Ravi Teja M
reviews.text: One of the beautiful islands you can visit during your day visits of your Halong Bay cruise. Theres a man made beach as well. The same is soo fine and theres a small hike on to a hill and you can experience great views of Halong Bay and all cruises.

reviews.author: Sing Wee Choo
reviews.text: The climb up Ti Top Island offers the best vantage point of Ha Long Bay. It consists of proper stone steps all the way up, equipped with helpful rope railings. You don't need hiking boots; doing it in sandals is completely fine. ​There is a mid-point break, but I recommend you don’t bother stopping there for pictures. Save your energy for the summit as the view from the top is way better. Be prepared that the path can get very crowded at some points depending on your timing. As for the beach below, it is quite small and not quite as beautiful as others so feel free to give it a miss and focus on the hike.

reviews.author: Manish _
reviews.text: What an amazing place. Natural beauty at its best. This island offers a small beach and hike to the top (decent climb) gives amazing views of Halong bay. the beauty memory is forever entrenched in my mind.

reviews.author: mandewi
reviews.text: The stairs was so steep you need a lot of pause when climbing up. You can make it tho. And the view is stunning. Not much at the top, the viewing area also not too large, so maybe you dont take too much time there.

reviews.author: Ibrahim Bathish
reviews.text: Never miss out on the breathtaking view of Halong Bay from this TITOP island, which requires you to climb 400 steps. Read the instructions before climbing, as it is not an easy climb. Take care while climbing the stairs.

reviews.author: Jane
reviews.text: We booked this as part of Ha Long bay cruise but we stayed at the boat since I saw so many vlogs that this is tiring. The views itself can be seen even when you're at the boat so I didn't want to climb so high and get tired lol.

reviews.author: Clive Coogan
reviews.text: Wow, what a view and that was only half way up! Sadly my camera was flat when I got to the Temple at the top. To get there go on Indochina sails with Mr Dai, as your host. Its his boat in the foreground shot😉

reviews.author: Collin Ng
reviews.text: Tobe updated... An island named in honour of the russian astronaut. Long flight of 300+ steps to the top, but not sure if it's worth it. 😅 Didn't have much time after that to walk around before we had to go back to the boat.

reviews.author: Jeraldine T
reviews.text: Scenic view but not absolutely breathtaking to the point I found worth climbing up and down 400 steep steps - yes there’s railings but think about having to manoeuvre your way through 2 way traffic - people climbing up and climbing down the same path- which honestly doesn’t make sense, and can be dangerous if it rains

reviews.author: Cyril's Outdoorology
reviews.text: Ti Top Island is a must-visit spot in Ha Long Bay. The climb to the viewpoint is a bit of a workout, but the panoramic views of the bay with its limestone karsts and turquoise waters are absolutely worth it. The beach at the base is small but perfect for a quick swim or relaxing by the water. It’s a beautiful combination of adventure, scenery, and relaxation — a highlight of any Ha Long Bay trip.

reviews.author: Stevent Febriandy
reviews.text: Visiting Ti Top Island in Ha Long Bay offers truly stunning panoramic views from the top, showcasing the iconic karst islands scattered across emerald waters. The scenery itself is amazing and clearly one of the highlights of Ha Long Bay. However, the experience was affected by heavy crowds. The route to the viewpoint has only one path for both going up and down, causing long queues and congestion. With only one hour allocated by the cruise, it became stressful to manage time, and many visitors eventually gave up before reaching the top. The beach area is decent, but again, overcrowding made it difficult to relax or fully enjoy the surroundings. Overall, the views are spectacular, but better crowd management and timing would greatly improve the experience.

reviews.author: G B
reviews.text: It's overfull and underserviced. Even at 07:45 on a Tuesday, the staircase to the top was overcrowded. The toilets are horrifyingly unsanitary. The showers seem permanently closed. The beach stinks of sewerage (because grey water is used to water the plants along the perimeter) and is visibly polluted - surprisingly few people get in the water. The view from the top is excellent and the stairs have a mostly even surface, but as an attraction which asks an admission fee, it deserves to be boycott.

reviews.author: Mohamed Aslam
reviews.text: This is a beautiful island that we visited as part of our Ha Long Bay cruise. It has over 400 steps to climb, which can be quite challenging, but once you reach the top you’re rewarded with stunning panoramic views of the bay.

reviews.author: Anilgowda
reviews.text: A worthwhile stop in Ha Long Bay for its iconic viewpoint and quick beach break, hiking but timing matters to enjoy it comfortably. But had a good time

reviews.author: Anujith Vijayakumar
reviews.text: Ti Top Island is an absolute highlight and a must-do on any Ha Long Bay itinerary. While the crescent-shaped beach with its soft white sand and clear turquoise water is perfect for a refreshing swim, the real magic is at the summit. ​The hike up the 400 steps is a little challenging, but the reward is a breathtaking, panoramic 360-degree view of the entire bay. Seeing the towering limestone karsts scattered across the emerald water from that vantage point is truly unforgettable and provides the best photo opportunities.

reviews.author: Dima Abramov
reviews.text: The most beautiful view on Hà Long Bay area is this island. The climb of 400 totally worth it

reviews.author: Paul C.
reviews.text: A relatively new beach with great views all around. The climb up to the top of the hill was steep, but you get one of the best views up there! The only big issue was the immense crowd around the beach area, from the various cruises. There are just too many tourists around to pick a spot to chill. Visited in April 2025.

reviews.author: Prakash Natarajan
reviews.text: Ti Top Island is one of the most popular tourist destinations in Ha Long Bay. I enjoyed swimming at its beautiful white-sand beach and climbed the 400 steep steps to the summit for breathtaking panoramic views of the bay. It was hard work to climb on a hot and humid day, but the stunning scenery made it well worth the effort.At the water’s edge were more of those unsafe, wet, narrow and steep stairs (no railings) that most people needed assistance with in order to get off and back on the boat (us included). The views were very nice but really not worth that much effort for so little time we had here which was only 20 minutes! The small beach was over-crowded with tourists from other boats as well as from ours (the Symphony).With the smaller boat we sailed to this island located a short distance from the cruise ship. Downstairs is a small sandy beach where non-walkers can enjoy the beach while the others take the +- 630 stairs up to enjoy the view over the bay.

reviews.author: Lou
reviews.text: I advise against visiting this island; it's a prime example of the disaster of mass tourism. The beach reeks of gasoline, and the water is polluted with gasoline and trash. Good luck getting to the viewpoint—not that the hike is difficult, but it's so crowded you have to queue just to move one step, only to end up crammed in at the top and unable to enjoy the view because you're blocking other people's photo sessions. This island has no appeal whatsoever. Tourists are packed in like sardines, and for goodness' sake, enjoy the view with your own eyes and live in the moment instead of visiting places just to take perfect photos for social media. It's so sad...

reviews.author: Sakban Rosidi
reviews.text: The activity offered was a light hike, but I was hesitant about going up all that before exploring Ha Long Bay and exploring the stalactite caves. So I just relaxed, took photos, and watched some tourists play beach volleyball.

reviews.author: Alina Martha Muresan
reviews.text: Great island !! The view from the peak is amazing 🔝 A must visit place during Halong Bay boat trip. Relaxing small sand beach. Reaching the peak in 20 mins climbing up the stairs. Worth it ! ✅ Visited January 2025

reviews.author: Kasif Malvat
reviews.text: It's great island with all activity like hiking with stairs, beach, swimming, cruise

reviews.author: Den Leo
reviews.text: Ti Top Island is one of the most popular tourist destinations in Ha Long Bay. I enjoyed swimming at its beautiful white-sand beach and climbed the 400 steep steps to the summit for breathtaking panoramic views of the bay. It was hard work to climb on a hot and humid day, but the stunning scenery made it well worth the effort.
